titleStmt.title: Miracles of Mary with ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Office for the consecration of an image or statue of Mary

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msItem.title: Taʾammǝra Māryām
Maṣḥafa śǝrʿāt za-waṣʾa ʾǝm-manbara Mārqos ḥawāryā ʾǝm-makāna maʿāllǝqā
msItem.note: See the text of the preface in Catalogus codicum manuscriptorum orientalium qui in Museo Britannico asservantur<hi rend="italics">, III:</hi> Codices Aethiopicos amplectensChristian Friedrich AugustDillmannn. p.E Museo Britannico1847https://reader.digitale-sammlungen.de/de/fs1/object/display/bsb10798621_00005.html26a.

msItem.title: Taʾammǝra Māryām
msItem.incipit: በስመ፡ ንቅድም፡ በረድኤተ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ። ወእምዝ፡ ንጽሕፍ፡ ተአምሪሃ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ቅድስት፡ ድንግል፡ በክልኤ፡ ማርያም፡ ዘበዕብራይጢ፡ ማሪሃም፡ ወላዲተ፡ አምላክ። ዘኢንክል፡ ፈጽሞ፡ ነጊረ፡ ዕበያ፡ ወክብራ፡

bibl.title: Aethiopische Marienhymnen
Abhandlungen der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften

series.t:title: Abhandlungen der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften
series.t:biblScope: 68, Abhandlungen der philologisch-historischen Klasse der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften, 33/4

bibl.biblScope: 68, Abhandlungen der philologisch-historischen Klasse der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften, 33/4

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo
Corresponding stanza of ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of the corresponding stanza of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor monk whom Mary gave a garment
Corresponding stanza of ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of the corresponding stanza of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 2, referring to
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza2. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jew of ʾAḫmīm
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 3, referring to
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza3. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 4, referring to
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza4. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 5, referring to
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza5. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 6, referring to
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza6. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza7. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza8. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza12. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza13. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza14. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza15. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor widow and her son who became a thief and was crucified
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 30, referring to ,
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza30. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 31, referring to
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza31. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the thirsty dog whom the Virgin gave water
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 32, referring to
msItem.note: The miracle ends with the first words of No item: LIT4274AkkonuB#Stanza32. The stanza is written in its entirety at the bottom of the page.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the preservation of the Church of the Virgin in ʾAṯrīb
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ንጉሥ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ከሊፋ፡ ዘነግሠ፡ ድኅረ፡ አቡሁ፡ ረሲድ። ወትርጓሜ፡ ስሙ፡ ድሩክ፡ ብእሲ፡ ብሒል። ወኮነ፡ እምቅድመ፡ ይትወፈይ፡ መንግሥተ፡ ይትቄየሞሙ፡ ለክርስቲያን፡

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How an image of the Virgin Mary wept for the sins of the world
Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary obtained entry to paradise for the soul of a monk who had kept her fast annually
Miracle of Mary: Homily on a potter from Syria
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a city destroyed by angels
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a man cursed by another
Miracle of Mary: Homily on the founding of the Convent of Mǝṭmāq
Miracle of Mary: Homily on a miracle at the Convent of Mǝṭmāq
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the water reservoir at the Convent of Mǝṭmāq
Miracle of Mary: Apparition of Jesus Christ to the Apostles, telling them where to build a church in honour of the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Christ makes St Peter head of the Church
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of an Arab chief sparing Christian pilgrims
Miracle of Mary: Homily on a European man who stole the door of the cave in Bethlehem
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the priest Rizqallā from Cairo by whose prayer stolen belongings of pilgrims where given back
Miracle of Mary: Five Pains of the Virgin, Conversation of the Virgin and Jesus Christ; promise of salvation to those who recite the Paternoster and Ave Maria
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the widow Martha and the monk Theodor of Ṣeʾednāyā
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a European man who cut a piece of a miraculous picture in Ṣeʾednāyā
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Theodosia, sister of Constantine, who found the cross in Jerusalem
Miracle of Mary: The man who wanted to give a banquet on St Mary's feast but was unable because of a famine
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the healing of a handicapped man
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of an abbot who died after refusing the reading of the Miracles of Mary
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a rich man who cast a loaf to a beggar and was saved by the Virgin after being condemned by Jesus
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Ṗeṭros Bawārǝs who demanded to know the mystery of the trinity after building a Church to the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a pagan woman of Rome who converted
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a man of Cappadocia whose child was resuscitated after prayer
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Martha, daughter of Fasilides, and her son Victor
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of St Minas
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Mark, son of the king of Rome, Gabra ʾIyasus
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the patriarch Denis who asked from the Virgin to know the grave of Mark
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of ʿAbd al-Masīḥ and Markezā
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Yoḥannǝs Kǝmā who founded a Convent
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the monk Yoḥannǝs (?) of St Macarius who had visions of the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of ʾabbā Matewos who wounded his tongue to escape from being nominated patriarch
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the drunk monk Timotewos (?), saved by the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the two monks Sadaqā and Ṗeṭros (?) of the Convent of St Victor
Miracle of Mary: How the image of the Virgin Mary in a Church bowed to the monks
msItem.incipit: ተብህለ፡ ሶበ፡ ፈቀዱ፡ አኀው፡ ከመ፡ ይትጋብኡ፡ ውስተ፡ መንበሊስ፡ ወተንሥኡ፡ እምአብርስፊስ፡ ኀበ፡ መንበሊስ፡

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a woman from Ceasarea whom Mary helped to give birth
Miracle of Mary: How an Ishmaelite was converted when he saw oil and milk flowing from the breasts of a picture of the Virgin Mary
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the monk ʾAbsādi of the Convent of ʾAbbā Sinoda
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Roman monk ʾOḍ of the Convent of Kwǝzārā
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Convent of Māḫǝw
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ኢየሩሳሌም፡ መንገለ፡ ሄኖን፡ በማዕዶተ፡ ዮርዳኖስ፡ ደብር፡

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor man who wanted to build a Chapel to Mary and of the buried pot of gold pieces
Miracle of Mary: St Mary comes to a church in the form of a white dove to heal from smallpox a man whose only good work was celebrating her commemoration
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor woman innocently accused of having killed her child
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a bandit saved by Mary after having stolen from churches
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the flight of Egypt; Miracle of the sycomore of Dabra Mǝṭmāq
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a city in Egypt destroyed by the curse of the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the source caused by Jesus and healing by its water
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the inhabitants of a city in Egypt transformed in monkeys and hedgehogs by the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary when at Bethlehem healed king Safron and drove out devils
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a woman and her family transformed into animals after having mistreated the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a fountain of water caused by Jesus in Māḫḍāb while returning to Palestine
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin announced by Gabriel
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, healings in Jerusalem
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, Jews burned in Bethlehem
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, the apostles convene around the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, Tāfonyā is sent to break the Virgin's bed and is saved by her
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, apparition of Jesus on Sunday among angels
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, Jesus separated the Virgin's soul from her body
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, the Virgin delivers condemned souls from hell
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, meeting with St John
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, the Jews threaten to burn the Virgin's body and cut off the heads of the apostles
Miracle of Mary: St John sees the Virgin in glory
Miracle of Mary: The Virgin, Apostles and others receive communion by Jesus
Miracle of Mary: The Virgin demands the announcement of her transmigration
Miracle of Mary: St John sees the Virgin in Heaven
Miracle of Mary: Miracles of the apparition of the Virgin to a sculptor and St Basil
Miracle of Mary: Narration of St Basil on the apparition of the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Narration about St Basil, on a woman who killed her sister
Miracle of Mary: Narration about John Chrysostom who defended Jesus' birth from the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Apparition of the Virgin to John Chrysostom
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a statue of the Virgin built by Arcadius
Office for the consecration of an image or statue of the Virgin
msItem.note: Written in a more recent hand.

item.desc: The original owner was Zamanfas Qǝddus. His name has later been scratched in most of the invocations and been replaced by those of ʿAśrāta Ṣǝyon, Walda Ṣǝyon and his wife Walatta ʾAmlāk.

bibl.title: Catalogue des manuscrits éthiopiens <hi rend="italics">(</hi>gheez et amharique<hi rend="italics">)</hi> de la Bibliothèque nationale
Manuscrits Orientaux
Aethiopische Marienhymnen
Abhandlungen der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften

series.t:title: Abhandlungen der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften
series.t:biblScope: 68, Abhandlungen der philologisch-historischen Klasse der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften, 33/4

bibl.biblScope: 68, Abhandlungen der philologisch-historischen Klasse der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften, 33/4

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

div.ab: በቤተ፡ ነጋድያን፡ በደብረ፡ ሕሙማን፡ መንገለ፡ ምሥራቅ፡
አሐቲ፡ ብእሲት፡ እምአንስተ፡ መኳንንተ፡ ሮሜ፡ ከነናዊት፡
ወሰመየቶ፡ ስሞ፡ ሚናስ፡ ዘበትርጓሜሁ፡ ምእመን፡ ቡሩክ። እስመ፡ ነሥአቶ፡ ለዝንቱ፡ ስም፡ እም፡ አሜን፡
ቦእምኔሆሙ፡ ዘኮነ፡ ሐልስትዮ፡ ዘገዳም። ወቦ፡ ከመ፡ ቅድር፡ ዘውእቱ፡ ቅንፍዝ።
ወበጽሑ፡ ሀገረ፡ ማኅርቃ። ወእምህየ፡ ሀገረ፡ ምሥር። ወእምህየ፡ ሀገረ፡ ማጥርያ፡ ወእምህየ፡ ሀገረ፡ ምኅፃብ።
ሰመየቶ፡ ለዮሐንስ፡ አፈ፡ ወርቅ፡ አመ፡ ሰዓመ፡ ኀፍረተ፡ ለብእሲት፡ ትከት፡ በእንተ፡ ክብረ፡ እግዚእትነ፡ ቅድስት፡ ድንግል።
